questions.text: Bile is used in:
questions.subject: Biology

Starch digestion is done by starch-digesting enzymes. Those are not present in the bile. The main starch-digesting enzyme is amylase, which is produced in the salivary glands and the pancreas.

Fat emulsification is done by bile salts, which are present in bile. Bile salts are compounds produced by the liver from cholesterol that are critical for fat digestion and the absorption of fat-soluble vitamins.
Both option A and B are incorrect.

questions.explanation: Bile juice (containing bile salts) is secreted from the liver, stored in the gallbladder, and secreted into the intestine via the bile duct. Bile salts are compounds produced by the liver from cholesterol that are critical for fat digestion and the absorption of fat-soluble vitamins. They are stored in the gallbladder and released into the small intestine after a meal. As amphipathic molecules, they have both water-soluble and fat-soluble parts, allowing them to break down large fat globules into smaller droplets, a process called emulsification. This increases the surface area for digestive enzymes to break down fats more effectively.

questions.text: Hybrid black Guinea pigs are crossed with each other. The resulting offsprings will be:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: There is a chance of white offspring (bb), so not all will be black.
White is recessive and appears only when both alleles are "b", which happens in only 25% of cases.

Hybrid black Guinea pigs have the genotype Bb, where B (black) is dominant and b (white) is recessive. When two Bb individuals are crossed, the Punnett square results in the following combinations:BB (Black) – 1/4 (25%)Bb (Black) – 1/2 (50%)bb (White) – 1/4 (25%)Thus, out of four offspring, three will be black (BB or Bb), and one will be white (bb).
This would be true if white were dominant, but black is dominant in this case.

questions.text: The enzyme in breast milk that causes the coagulation of milk or forms precipitates of milk is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Rennin, also known as chymosin, is an enzyme that plays a key role in the coagulation of milk proteins, particularly casein. It is commonly found in the stomach lining of young mammals, including human infants. The primary function of rennin is to curdle or coagulate milk, forming a semisolid mass. This coagulation is important for the digestion and absorption of milk proteins in the stomach.

Trypsin is another digestive enzyme, but it plays a role in the digestion of proteins in the small intestine, not in the coagulation of milk. Trypsin is produced by the pancreas and released into the small intestine, where it acts to break down proteins into smaller peptides.

Amylase is an enzyme that plays a crucial role in the digestion of carbohydrates. There are two main types of amylase: salivary amylase, which is produced in the salivary glands, and pancreatic amylase, which is produced in the pancreas. Both types of amylase work to break down complex carbohydrates, such as starch, into simpler sugars like maltose.

Lipase is an enzyme that plays a crucial role in the digestion of fats (lipids). It is produced by the pancreas and released into the small intestine, where it works to break down dietary fats into smaller molecules, such as fatty acids and glycerol. This process is essential for the absorption of fats in the digestive system.

questions.explanation: Rennin, also known as chymosin, is an enzyme that plays a key role in the coagulation of milk proteins, particularly casein. It is commonly found in the stomach lining of young mammals, including human infants. The primary function of rennin is to curdle or coagulate milk, forming a semisolid mass. This coagulation is important for the digestion and absorption of milk proteins in the stomach.

questions.text: The egg-laying birds are called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct. The term oviparous comes from the Latin words “ovi” (meaning egg) and “parous” (meaning to give birth or produce)*. Oviparous animals lay eggs, and the embryos develop outside the mother’s body. The eggs usually have a protective shell or coating to prevent desiccation and provide nutrients to the developing embryo. All birds are oviparous because they lay eggs that hatch outside their bodies. Examples include hens, sparrows, pigeons, and eagles. This strategy allows birds to produce multiple offspring at once and is an adaptation suited to their mode of life.

This option is incorrect. Viviparous animals are those that give birth to live young ones instead of laying eggs. The embryos develop inside the mother’s body, and the young are nourished directly by the mother, often through a placenta (as in most mammals). Examples include humans, dogs, and cows. Since birds lay eggs, they are not viviparous.

This option is incorrect. Monotremes are a special group of egg-laying mammals (class Prototheria) — not birds. They are unique because, unlike other mammals, they reproduce by laying eggs. Examples include the platypus and the echidna. Although monotremes lay eggs like birds, the question specifically asks about birds, so this option does not fit.

This option is incorrect, because only oviparous animals (option A) correctly describe egg-laying birds. The other two terms — viviparous and monotremes — refer to different types of reproduction in animals.

questions.explanation: Oviparous is producing young by means of eggs that are hatched after they have been laid by the parent, as in birds. Viviparous means bringing forth live young who have developed inside the body of the parent. Monotreme is a primitive mammal that lays large yolky eggs and has a common opening for the urogenital and digestive systems.

options.explanation: Hydra is a small, freshwater organism belonging to the phylum Cnidaria. It has both internal and external digestion.
Jellyfish, also known as jelly sea jellies or medusae, belong to the phylum Cnidaria. They predominantly rely on external digestion.
Flatworms, specifically the class Turbellaria, are a group of non-parasitic worms belonging to the phylum Platyhelminthes. They exhibit a combination of internal and external digestion.
Hydra, jellyfish, and flatworms, specifically the class, all demonstrate both internal and external digestion to varying degrees.

questions.explanation: Internal digestion occurs when food is digested within the body of the organism, while external digestion is when an organism secretes digestive enzymes outside its body to digest food material and then absorb the digestive nutrients. Hydra, jellyfish, and flatworms all show a combination of these methods where they secrete enzymes, ingest partially digested food then internally digest the remaining.

questions.text: Milk-drinking babies have an additional enzyme called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Rennin, also known as chymosin, is an enzyme found in the stomachs of young mammals, including human infants. It plays a crucial role in the digestion of milk proteins, specifically casein. Rennin helps coagulate milk, allowing it to be more easily digested by breaking down casein into smaller, more digestible peptides.

Amylase is an enzyme that helps in the digestion of carbohydrates. It breaks down complex carbohydrates into smaller sugar molecules, such as glucose, for absorption. While amylase is involved in the digestion process, it is not specific to milk and does not play a direct role in milk digestion.

Lipase is an enzyme responsible for breaking down fats or lipids. It helps in the digestion and absorption of dietary fats by breaking them down into smaller molecules called fatty acids and glycerol. Like amylase, lipase is not specific to milk and is not directly involved in milk digestion.

options.text: None of these options are correct.
options.explanation: Incorrect option, as option A is correct.

questions.explanation: Milk-drinking babies have an additional enzyme rennin because it causes solidification of the milk, and solid milk is digested slowly, so even if they are on a milk-only diet, they feel less hungry.

questions.text: Egg-laying mammals are called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Prototheria is a subclass of mammals that includes egg-laying mammals like the platypus and echidna.
Protozoa are single-celled microscopic organisms, not mammals.
Chordata is a large phylum that includes all animals with a notochord, including mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians, and fish. It is too broad to specifically refer to egg-laying mammals.
Monotremes are a group of egg-laying mammals within the subclass Prototheria. Examples include the platypus and echidnas.
Since Prototheria and Monotremes both refer to egg-laying mammals, this is the most accurate choice.

questions.explanation: Monotremes lay eggs and prototheria are both egg-laying mammals so the question has two correct answers. Chordata, the classification to which humans belong, are those that do not lay eggs but rather give birth to young ones. Protozoa, on the other hand, are unicellular organisms.

questions.text: Aerobic respiration results in how much ATP?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect as aerobic respiration does not yield 2 ATP. Anaerobic respiration yields 2 ATP

Aerobic respiration involves multiple stages: glycolysis, the citric acid cycle (also known as the Krebs cycle), and the electron transport chain. In the presence of oxygen, these processes occur in the mitochondria. In total, one molecule of glucose can produce a net gain of 36 ATP molecules through aerobic respiration.
Option C is incorrect as aerobic respiration yields 36 ATP, not 18.
Option D is incorrect as aerobic respiration yields 36 ATP, not 32.

questions.explanation: In eukaryotic cells, aerobic respiration typically results in a net gain of approximately 36 ATP molecules per molecule of glucose. This energy yield is a sum of ATP produced during glycolysis, the citric acid cycle (Krebs cycle), and oxidative phosphorylation in the electron transport chain.

questions.text: Which process takes place during the movement of glucose from the body fluid to the blood?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Endosmosis is the movement of water molecules into a cell across a semipermeable membrane. This usually occurs when the surrounding solution is hypotonic, causing water to flow into the cell to balance solute concentrations.

Osmosis is the passive movement of water across a semipermeable membrane from a region of higher water concentration to a region of lower water concentration. It helps maintain cell turgor and balance solute levels inside and outside the cell.

Active transport is the movement of molecules across a cell membrane against their concentration gradient, requiring energy in the form of ATP or coupled ion gradients. In the case of glucose moving from intestinal cells to blood, it moves down its concentration gradient through carrier proteins, so it does not use energy. That’s why active transport is incorrect for this step.

The movement of glucose from intestinal fluid into the blood is mostly through facilitated diffusion. Inside intestinal cells, glucose enters actively (with sodium co-transport). But from the intestinal cell to the blood, glucose moves by facilitated diffusion, through carrier proteins (like GLUT2) without using ATP.

questions.explanation: The movement of glucose from intestinal fluid into the blood is mostly through facilitated diffusion. Inside intestinal cells, glucose enters actively (with sodium co-transport). But from the intestinal cell to the blood, glucose moves by facilitated diffusion, through carrier proteins (like GLUT2) without using ATP.

questions.text: The ecological succession starting from drylands is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ecological succession initiated in dry or bare land is called xerarch and the collective stages are called xerosere.
Hydrophytes refer to freshwater plants.
Halophytes are the plants that live in saltwater.
Only one of the above options is correct.

questions.explanation: Xerarch succession takes place in dry areas and the series progress from xeric to mesic conditions. In ecology, a mesic habitat is a type of habitat with a moderate or well-balanced supply of moisture.

questions.text: Organs which produce voice in birds is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Larynx is the voice box in humans.
Pharynx is the passageway leading from the nasal cavity to oesophagus and voice box.
Syrinx is the organ of sound production in birds.
Option A is incorrect, birds have no larynx.

questions.explanation: Organs of voice production in birds are called syrinx.

questions.text: Treponema pallidum causes:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct.Syphilis is a sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum, which is a spirochaete bacterium (spiral-shaped and motile). The infection usually spreads through sexual contact but can also be transmitted from mother to baby during pregnancy (congenital syphilis). The disease progresses in four stages — primary, secondary, latent, and tertiary syphilis — each with different symptoms.Primary stage: painless sores (chancres) on the genitals.Secondary stage: skin rashes and mucous membrane lesions.Latent stage: infection becomes hidden with no visible symptoms.Tertiary stage: may cause serious damage to organs such as the brain, heart, and nerves.Treatment involves antibiotics, mainly penicillin.

This option is incorrect.Gonorrhoea is another sexually transmitted disease (STD), but it is caused by a different bacterium, Neisseria gonorrhoeae, not Treponema pallidum. The infection affects the urethra, cervix, rectum, and throat, and can cause painful urination and discharge from genital organs. Although both gonorrhoea and syphilis are STDs, they are caused by different pathogens and have different clinical features.

This option is incorrect.AIDS (Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome) is caused by the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV), not by bacteria. HIV attacks and weakens the immune system, making the body unable to fight infections. It is also sexually transmitted but belongs to a completely different category of pathogens — viruses, not bacteria like Treponema pallidum.

This option is incorrect.Herpes is caused by the Herpes simplex virus (HSV), not by Treponema pallidum. There are two main types: HSV-1, which causes oral herpes (cold sores), and HSV-2, which causes genital herpes. The infection leads to painful blisters and sores around the mouth or genitals. It is viral, not bacterial, and thus unrelated to Treponema pallidum.

questions.explanation: Syphilis is a sexually transmitted infectious (STI) disease caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum.

questions.text: Lamarck is best known for his theory of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect as theory of inheritance was the name given to Mendel’s theory.
Option B is incorrect as there are no scientific mentions of the theory of dominance. We only know the laws of dominance.
Lamarck explained evolution using his theory 'Inheritance of acquired characteristics'. This theory was later disproved so option C is correct.
Option D is incorrect as only Option C is correct.

questions.explanation: Lamarck is best known for his Theory of Inheritance of Acquired Characteristics, first presented in 1809 (Darwin's first book dealing with natural selection was published in 1859): If an organism changes during life in order to adapt to its environment, those changes are passed on to its offspring.

questions.text: What is the commercial method of producing millions of seedlings in limited time?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Parthenogenesis is a reproductive strategy where an egg develops into an organism without fertilization, typically seen in certain invertebrates. It is not a method used in commercial seedling production.

Parthenocarpy refers to the development of fruit without fertilization, resulting in seedless fruits. While it has applications in agriculture, it does not pertain to the mass production of seedlings.

Micropropagation is a sophisticated tissue culture technique that allows for the rapid production of thousands to millions of genetically identical seedlings in a controlled environment, making it an efficient commercial method.

Grafting involves joining parts from two different plants to grow as one, often for traits like disease resistance. However, it is not designed for the mass production of seedlings and is slower compared to micropropagation.

questions.explanation: Micropropagation is a tissue culture technique used commercially to produce millions of identical seedlings in a short time under controlled conditions.

questions.text: Cell wall is made up of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is correct as plants have a cell wall that comprises mainly of cellulose.
Option B is incorrect as tissues are made up of groups of cells.
Option C is incorrect as ribosomes are the site of protein synthesis in a cell and are not linked to the cell wall.
Option D is incorrect as penicillin-binding protein does not make the cell wall as they are a group of proteins that are characterized by their affinity for and binding of penicillin.

questions.explanation: The cell wall is synthesized by cellulose which is a polymer of beta-glucose.

questions.text: The cells are held together by a Ca-pectate layer are called as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The primary cell wall is present outermost to the cell. It contains cellulose and hemicellulose in a criss cross manner
A secondary cell wall is present between the plasma membrane and the primary cell wall. It contains waxes, salts, silica and mainly lignin which makes it harder.
The middle layer (made of Ca-pectate) is present between adjacent plant cells.
There is no tertiary cell wall in plants.

questions.explanation: The plant cell is surrounded by cellulosic cell walls. The cell walls of two adjacent cells are separated by middle lamella which literally means middle plate or middle membrane. Middle lamella is actually the cementing layer between two plant cells. It is absent on the outer surface. It ruptures to create intercellular spaces wherever required. It is formed of calcium and magnesium pectate.

questions.text: Thyroid gland requires high amount of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phosphate is involved in bone and teeth formation.
Calcium is involved in bone formation and muscle contraction.
Iodine is a component of thyroid hormones Triiodothyronine and Tetraiodothyronine.
Sodium is involved in nerve impulse conduction.

questions.explanation: Iodine is essential to maintain a healthy thyroid. Iodine is the critical 'ingredient' for the production of thyroid hormones.

questions.text: Which of the following is not the function of the cerebrum?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect as the functions of the cerebrum include interpreting memory, reasoning, judgment, and thoughts, as well as coordinating movement. It is not involved in digestion. Additionally, digestion is not a voluntary process.
The cerebrum is heavily involved in thinking processes. It is responsible for complex cognitive functions, including reasoning, problem-solving, decision-making, and creativity.
Intelligence is closely related to the functions of the cerebrum. The cerebrum, particularly the cerebral cortex, is responsible for higher-order thinking and intellectual abilities.
The cerebrum is involved in the voluntary control of skeletal muscles. It sends signals through the motor cortex to initiate and coordinate voluntary movements.

questions.explanation: Functions of the cerebrum include initiation of movement, coordination of movement, temperature, touch, vision, hearing, judgment, reasoning, problem-solving, emotions, and learning, which do not involve digestion. Digestion is controlled by the autonomic nervous system- it is not a voluntary function!

questions.text: Which of the following is the function of adrenaline?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: To increase the rate of breathing
options.explanation: Adrenaline causes bronchial dilation which results in faster breathing.
Adrenaline causes vasoconstriction which leads to an increase in heart rate.

options.text: To increase the level of calcium in blood
options.explanation: The level of calcium in the blood is increased by the action of parathormone.
Both option A and B are correct.

questions.explanation: Adrenaline is released in a fight or flight situation. In flight or fight conditions, both the breathing rate and the heart rate increase in order to increase alertness.

questions.text: Antibodies are actually:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Antibodies are globular proteins that play a key role in the immune system by binding to antigens. Their compact, three-dimensional structure enables them to specifically recognize and neutralize foreign substances.

Glycoproteins are proteins that have carbohydrate chains (glycans) attached to them. While some proteins in the body, including antibodies, can be glycosylated (have carbohydrates attached), the primary description for antibodies is their classification as globular proteins.

Fibrous proteins have elongated, thread-like shapes and are often involved in structural roles. Examples of fibrous proteins include collagen and keratin. Antibodies do not fall under the category of fibrous proteins due to their globular shape and function.

Glycolipids are molecules composed of both carbohydrates and lipids. They are typically found in cell membranes and serve various roles, including cell recognition. Antibodies are not glycolipids; they are globular proteins involved in immune responses.

questions.explanation: Antibodies are globular proteins that play a key role in the immune system by binding to antigens. Their compact, three-dimensional structure enables them to specifically recognize and neutralize foreign substances.

questions.text: Hepatic and pancreatic secretions are also stimulated by a hormone called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Gastrin is a hormone produced by the stomach that primarily stimulates gastric acid secretion and promotes gastric motility. While it plays a critical role in digestion, it does not directly stimulate hepatic or pancreatic secretions.

Secretin is a hormone secreted by the small intestine (specifically, the duodenum) in response to acidic chyme. It plays a vital role in regulating the pH of the small intestine by stimulating the pancreas to release bicarbonate and promoting bile production in the liver, making it essential for hepatic and pancreatic secretions.

Hydrochloric acid (HCl) is produced by the parietal cells of the stomach and is crucial for digestion and maintaining an acidic environment. However, it is not a hormone and does not stimulate hepatic or pancreatic secretions.

Enterokinase is an enzyme produced in the small intestine that activates pancreatic enzymes, specifically trypsinogen into trypsin, but it does not stimulate hepatic or pancreatic secretions directly.

questions.explanation: Secretin is the correct answer because it is a hormone released by the small intestine in response to acidic chyme. It stimulates the pancreas to secrete bicarbonate, which neutralizes stomach acid and promotes liver bile production, thus facilitating digestion. In contrast, gastrin primarily stimulates the stomach's activity; HCl is a gastric secretion rather than a hormone, and enterokinase is an enzyme that activates other digestive enzymes but does not stimulate secretion from the liver or pancreas.

questions.text: The respiratory pigment, which has a much higher affinity to combine with oxygen, is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Myoglobin, although being able to bind with only one oxygen, has a higher affinity for oxygen and only releases it at PO2 20 mmHg. For this reason, it is involved with oxygen storage.

Globin is the protein part of hemoglobin and does not bind oxygen. Instead, it can bind carbon dioxide to form carbaminohemoglobin, which helps transport CO2 from tissues to the lungs. Oxygen binds to the heme portion of hemoglobin, not the globin chains.

Hemoglobin does bind oxygen, but its affinity is lower than that of myoglobin, which allows myoglobin to store oxygen in muscle tissues. Hemoglobin starts releasing significant amounts of oxygen when the partial pressure of oxygen (PO₂) drops to around 60 mmHg (not mg). This helps deliver oxygen efficiently to tissues that need it.

Haemocyanin is another respiratory pigment found in some invertebrates, such as mollusks and arthropods. It also transports oxygen, but uses copper instead of iron like hemoglobin. Its oxygen affinity varies by species, but it generally has lower affinity than myoglobin, making its behavior more comparable to hemoglobin in releasing oxygen to tissues.

questions.explanation: Myoglobin is a pigment that has the highest affinity to bind with oxygen.

questions.text: Coelom is a cavity lined by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Coelom cavity is present in between the body wall and alimentary canal and is lined by Mesoderm.
The epidermis is the outermost layer of skin in vertebrates, and it is derived from the mesoderm, not Epiderm.
This option is also incorrect because coelom is a cavity lined by Mesoderm, not endoderm.
This option is also incorrect because the ectoderm is the outermost germ layer, and it gives rise to the epidermis, nervous system, and other tissues in the body.

questions.explanation: Explanation:The coelom cavity is present between the body wall and the alimentary canal and is lined by Mesoderm.OPTION B: The epidermis is the outermost layer of skin in vertebrates, and it is derived from the mesoderm, not the ectoderm.This option is also incorrect because the coelom is a cavity lined by Mesoderm, not endoderm.This option is also incorrect because the ectoderm is the outermost germ layer, and it gives rise to the epidermis, nervous system, and other tissues in the body.

questions.text: Which one of the following does not have intermediate host?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Tapeworms live in cattle before infecting humans.Tapeworms belong to the phylum Platyhelminthes, which includes all flatworms
Ascaris only spends its lifetime in one host.Ascaris lumbricoides is a large parasitic roundworm of the genus Ascaris. It is the most common parasitic worm in humans

Liver flukes lives in sheep before infecting liver of other organisms.Liver fluke is a collective name of a polyphyletic group of parasitic trematodes under the phylum Platyhelminthes. They are principally parasites of the liver of various mammals, including humans

Plasmodium lives in mosquitoes before infecting humans.Plasmodium is a genus of unicellular eukaryotes that are obligate parasites of vertebrates and insects. The life cycles of Plasmodium species involve development in a blood-feeding insect host which then injects parasites into a vertebrate host during a blood meal

questions.explanation: Organisms that have an intermediate host spend their lifetimes in two different organisms.Ascaris lumbricoides is a large parasitic roundworm of the genus Ascaris. It is the most common parasitic worm in humans

questions.text: Gymnosperms are referred to as "naked-seeded plants", because:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect as gymnosperms do have ovules but they are not enclosed in a ovary.

Yes, that statement is correct. Gymnosperms lack ovaries. Gymnosperms produce seeds that are not enclosed within ovaries. Instead, the ovules (structures containing female gametes or eggs) are exposed on the surface of cone scales.
Option C is incorrect as the embryo is enclosed in a protective barrier or seed coat.
Option D is incorrect as gymnosperms do have a seed coat for protection.

questions.explanation: Gymnosperms produce seeds that are not enclosed within ovaries. Instead, the ovules (structures containing female gametes or eggs) are exposed on the surface of cone scales. The term "gymnosperm" means "naked seed." The seeds of gymnosperms are not protected within a fruit, as is the case with angiosperms. Instead, they are often borne on the scales of cones.

questions.text: Immediate source of energy for cellular respiration is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Lipids need to be broken down in order to be utilised as a source of energy.Hence it is not an immediate source

The immediate source of energy for cellular respiration is adenosine triphosphate, or ATP. ATP is a molecule that stores and releases energy in cells. During cellular respiration, the complex organic molecules, such as glucose, are gradually broken down in a series of enzymatic reactions. The energy released during these reactions is used to synthesize ATP from adenosine diphosphate (ADP) and inorganic phosphate (Pi).
Proteins are also too complex and need to be broken down before being used as an energy source.
Even simple carbohydrates like glucose can not be immediately used by the cell, they need to be broken down by respiration to be used.

questions.explanation: The immediate source of energy for cellular respiration is adenosine triphosphate, or ATP. ATP is a molecule that stores and releases energy in cells. During cellular respiration, the complex organic molecules, such as glucose, are gradually broken down in a series of enzymatic reactions. The energy released during these reactions is used to synthesize ATP from adenosine diphosphate (ADP) and inorganic phosphate (Pi).

questions.text: Haemoglobin exhibits a:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Haemoglobin consists of 4 protein chains bound to each other.In the secondary structure of proteins, amino acids are folded or twisted by hydrogen bonds.
In the primary structure, a simple amino acid chain is present. While, hemoglobin is a complex structure. So, this option is not correct.
In quaternary structure, multiple proteins are held together by hydrophobic interactions. Hemoglobin consists of four polypeptide chains. So, this option is correct.
In tertiary structure disulfide linkages form, giving proteins a globular structure. So, this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: Haemoglobin has a quaternary structure. It consists of two pairs of different proteins, designated the α and β chains. There are 141 and 146 amino acids in the α and β chains of haemoglobin, respectively. As in myoglobin, each subunit is linked covalently to a molecule of heme.

questions.text: Arteriosclerosis is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Metabolic disorders are those that result in failed metabolism of substances such as Tay-Sachs disease. They are usually genetic.
Degenerative disorders are those in which the functioning of an organ is damaged or disrupted over time such as osteoporosis, and arteriosclerosis.
Infectious disorders are those in which a parasite such as a bacteria infects the body and causes disease.
Nutritional deficiency diseases are those in which lack of a nutrient in the diet causes its deficiency in the body.

questions.explanation: Arteriosclerosis is a disorder in which cholesterol and fat buildup in arteries cause their narrowing or blockage which leads to damaged arteries.Arteriosclerosis, literally means "hardening of the arteries" is characterized by abnormal thickening, hardening, and loss of elasticity of the walls of arteries.

questions.text: When phenotype of a heterozygote is in between the phenotypes of both the homozygous parents, it is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Incomplete dominance occurs when the phenotype of the heterozygous individual is intermediate (a blend) between the phenotypes of the two homozygous parents.In this case, neither allele is completely dominant over the other.For example, in snapdragon flowers, when a red-flowered plant (RR) is crossed with a white-flowered plant (rr), the F₁ generation has pink flowers (Rr).This shows that the heterozygote’s phenotype is a mixture of the two parental phenotypes — not purely one or the other.Thus, incomplete dominance reflects partial expression of both alleles, producing an intermediate trait.

Epistasis is not about blending traits, but about interaction between genes at different loci.In epistasis, one gene masks or suppresses the effect of another gene, even if both are dominant.For example, in Labrador retrievers, a gene for pigment deposition (E) can hide the effect of the color gene (B); if a dog is homozygous recessive (ee), it will be yellow regardless of its B/b genotype.Therefore, epistasis affects how genes interact, not how alleles within a single gene blend — so this does not describe the intermediate phenotype seen in incomplete dominance.

Pleiotropy is when a single gene influences multiple, seemingly unrelated traits.For instance, in sickle-cell anemia, the defective hemoglobin gene affects red blood cell shape, oxygen transport, and resistance to malaria — all different aspects controlled by a single gene.This is completely different from incomplete dominance, which deals with the expression of two alleles for one trait.So pleiotropy refers to one gene → many effects, not two alleles → blended phenotype.

Codominance occurs when both alleles in a heterozygote are fully expressed — not blended.In this case, both parental traits appear side by side in the phenotype rather than mixing.A common example is human blood group AB, where both A and B alleles are expressed equally on red blood cells.Thus, in codominance, both traits are visible simultaneously, whereas in incomplete dominance, the traits mix to form an intermediate appearance.

questions.explanation: This type of relationship between alleles, with a heterozygote phenotype intermediate between the two homozygous phenotypes, is called incomplete dominance. In the question, "in between" is closest in meaning to "intermediate", thus incomplete dominance is the correct choice. Thus, the correct answer is 'Diphtheria, Leprosy, Plague.'

questions.text: Cloning is a form of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Parthenogenesis occurs when an unfertilized cell develops into a haploid embryo in animals.
Apomixis is when an unfertilized ovule develops into a haploid embryo in plants.
Sexual reproduction is when haploid gametes (Male and female) join to form a diploid zygote that has characters of both parents.
Asexual reproduction is when an organism has offspring identical to it through mitosis or budding.

questions.explanation: Cloning occurs when the offspring is an exact copy of the parent.

questions.text: Evolutionary relationships amongst species are reflected in their:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: DNA is the main hereditary material that is passed down between generations. As such the DNA and the proteins encoded by it can give a clue about how closely related organisms are.
RNA does not have that much of a role in either Inheritance or the structure of the body so it has no role in determining relationships between organisms.
Genes are particular sequences of DNA.
While DNA does have a role in determining evolutionary relationships, RNA has no involvement in that.

questions.explanation: The extent to which proteins and DNA are conserved between organisms gives us clues about the amount of evolutionary time that has passed since the two organisms diverged from one another.

questions.text: The productivity of aquatic ecosystem is determined by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Water is necessary for the existence of aquatic ecosystems, but it is not a direct determinant of their productivity.
This option is correct. Light is necessary for photosynthesis to occur because it provides the energy to drive the process of converting carbon dioxide and water into glucose (sugar) and oxygen.

Nutrients such as nitrogen and phosphorus are necessary for the growth of primary producers such as algae, while light is necessary for photosynthesis to occur. The availability of these factors influences the productivity of the aquatic ecosystem.
This option is also correct. Nutrients such as nitrogen and phosphorus are necessary for the growth of primary producers such as algae.

questions.explanation: Nutrients such as nitrogen and phosphorus are necessary for the growth of primary producers such as algae, while light is necessary for photosynthesis to occur. The availability of these factors influences the productivity of the aquatic ecosystem. Water is necessary for the existence of aquatic ecosystems, but it is not a direct determinant of their productivity. Light is necessary for photosynthesis to occur. Nutrients such as nitrogen and phosphorus are necessary for the growth of primary producers such as algae.

questions.text: Diseases in living organisms which are caused by parasites are called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Disinfestation is the removal of a parasite or harmful organism.
Antisepsis is the removal of an infection or infectious agent.
Infection refers to growth or attack of microorganisms such as bacteria.
Infestation refers to an overgrowth of some organism, particularly a harmful one, in an area. Diseases caused by the overgrowth of these harmful parasites are called infestations.

questions.explanation: FACT Diseases in living organisms that are caused by parasites are called infestations.

questions.text: The technique used for the non-surgical removal of kidney stones is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ultrasound is a technique in which ultrasound waves are used go create an image of internal organs.

Lithotripsy, or extracorporeal shockwave lithotripsy is a technique in which the abdomen is bombarded with high-energy shock waves that cause the disintegration of kidney stones. The kidney stone dust is then excreted along with urine.
Dialysis is an artificial method of blood filtering that is used if kidney function fails.
X-ray is a technique in which X-rays are used to get an image of the bones inside the body.

questions.explanation: Extracorporeal shock wave lithotripsy is a technique for treating stones in the kidney and ureter that does not require surgery. Instead, high-energy shock waves are passed through the body and used to break stones into pieces as small as grains of sand.

questions.text: Microcephaly, the small sized skull is due to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Nutritional errors may cause weakening of bones but it doesn't cause the deformation or size difference in bones.
Skeletal defects can cause several disorders but the root cause of microcephaly is genetic cause which is also a cause of many skeletal errors.
Hormones control the deposition of material in bones but they do not affect the size or their formation.
The formation of bones is influenced mainly by genes so it is likely that errors in the formation of bones are due to genetic defects.

questions.explanation: Microcephaly is a condition where the head (circumference) is smaller than normal. Microcephaly may be caused by genetic abnormalities. Genetic mutations in various genes can disrupt brain development and cause microcephaly. Some well-known genetic syndromes associated with microcephaly include Down syndrome, Apert syndrome, and Seckel syndrome.

questions.text: The most abundant organic molecule on the planet earth is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Starch, glycogen, and glucose are also carbohydrates, but they are not as abundant as cellulose. Starch is a storage form of energy in plants.
Starch, glycogen, and glucose are also carbohydrates, but they are not as abundant as cellulose. Glycogen is a storage form of energy in animals.
Starch, glycogen, and glucose are also carbohydrates, but they are not as abundant as cellulose. Glucose is a simple sugar that is the main source of energy for cells.

Cellulose is an essential part of the plant cell wall so it is abundant in plants. Plants are spread throughout the earth so it makes cellulose the most abundant organic molecule. Cellulose is a polysaccharide, or carbohydrate, that is made up of glucose monomers. Cellulose is also a major component of wood and cotton. Hence, this option is correct.

questions.explanation: The most abundant organic molecule on the planet Earth is cellulose. Cellulose is a polysaccharide, or carbohydrate, that is made up of glucose monomers. It is found in the cell walls of plants and is the main structural component of plant fibers. Cellulose is also a major component of wood and cotton.

questions.text: The active sites of all enzyme are formed by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The amino acid chain in an enzyme forms foldings in such a way that particular R chains form the active site. The R chains in every amino acid are different so in different enzymes, different amino acid sequences form different active sites. Hence, this option is correct.
NH2 groups of amino acids are a constant part so they are the same in each enzyme. If they were part of active sites all the active sites would be the same.
COOH groups are also the same in each enzyme.
If disulphide bonds were present in active sites, only those substrates could attach that had sulphur in them. So only limited substrates could be catalyzed.

questions.explanation: The R group, also known as the side chain, is the part of an amino acid that determines its properties. The R groups of amino acids can be very different from each other, and they give enzymes their specificity for different substrates.

questions.text: Detoxification of drugs is the function of _ in a cell.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: RER is involved in protein synthesis because they have ribosomes on their surface which helps them in protein synthesis which is the passed to SER and then to golgi complex for modification.
The smooth endoplasmic reticulum is responsible for the detoxification of drugs within the cell. It is also responsible for metabolism of lipids and provides supports too.
Liver cells detoxify drugs from the whole body. Liver cells helps in the detoxification of urea mainly and other harmful material from the body but not in a cell.

questions.text: The molecule used by most of the animals for long-term energy storage is:

questions.explanation: The most commonly prescribed contraceptive pill is the combined hormonal pill with progesterone. Progesterone is primarily responsible for preventing pregnancy. Graphite is an allotrope of carbon and is known for its high electrical conductivity. The carbon atoms in graphite are arranged in a layered structure, forming sheets of hexagonal rings. These sheets have delocalized electrons that can move freely along the layers, allowing for efficient electron flow and high electrical conductivity. Therefore, solid graphite has a high electrical conductivity.

Solid sodium chloride, also known as table salt, is a crystalline structure composed of alternating sodium (Na+) and chloride (Cl-) ions. While ionic compounds like sodium chloride can conduct electricity when dissolved in water or molten state due to the movement of ions, they do not conduct electricity in the solid state. The fixed positions of the ions in the crystal lattice restrict the movement of charged particles, resulting in low electrical conductivity for solid sodium chloride.

Gaseous carbon dioxide consists of covalently bonded molecules. Since the molecules do not have a net charge and do not dissociate into ions in the gaseous state, gaseous carbon dioxide does not conduct electricity.

questions.text: In an adiabatic process:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Pressure is not maintained constant in an adiabatic process.
The gas is not isothermally expanded in an adiabatic process.

The adiabatic process is defined as a process in which no heat transfer takes place. This does not mean that the temperature is constant, but rather that no heat is transferred into or out of the system.

options.text: System exchanges heat with the surroundings
options.explanation: The system does not exchange heat with the surroundings because the heat exchange is zero.

questions.explanation: During an adiabatic process, the system is completely insulated from its surroundings. Thus, it takes place without heat entering or leaving the system, i.e.,q=0. During an isobaric process, the pressure of the system is kept constant (ΔP=0). During the isochoric process, the volume of the system is kept constant. (ΔV=0). During an isothermal process. the temperature of the system is kept constant (ΔT=0 but q≠0).

questions.text: Standard heat enthalpy of a compound is equal to its:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The standard enthalpy of combustion of the substance is the amount of heat evolved when one mole of the substance is completely burnt in excess of oxygen under standard conditions. It is denoted by ∆Hc. C2H5OH + 3O2 —>2CO2 + 3H2O H =-1368kJ / mol

The standard enthalpy of the formation of a compound is the amount of heat absorbed or evolved when one mole of the compound is formed from its elements. It is denoted by ∆H°f. All the substances involved are in their standard physical states and the reaction is carried out under standard conditions i.e. at 25°C (298 K) and one atm. Enthalpy of a compound = Heat of reaction of that compound

The standard enthalpy of a solution is the amount of heat absorbed or evolved when one mole of a substance is dissolved in so much solvent that further dilution results in no detectable heat change. For example, the enthalpy of solution (∆Ho sol.) of ammonium chloride is +16.2 kJmol-1 and that of sodium carbonate is -25.0 kJmol-1. In the first case, heat absorbed from the surroundings is indicated by cooling of the solvent (water), an endothermic process. While in the second case, the temperature of the solvent rises showing that the process is exothermic.

The enthalpy of dilution, also known as the heat of dilution, can be defined as the change in enthalpy that is associated with the dilution of a specific component of a solution when the pressure is kept constant.

questions.explanation: The enthalpy of reaction for any chemical reaction is equal to the difference between the sum of the enthalpies of the formation of the products and the sum of the enthalpies of the reactants. In an exothermic reaction, the heat content or enthalpy of the product H2 is less than that of the reactants H1. Since the system has lost heat, we can say the enthalpy change for the reaction ∆H is negative, In an endothermic reaction, the enthalpy of product H2 is greater than that of the reactants H1, and the enthalpy change, ∆H is positive. The standard enthalpy of a reaction ∆Ho is the enthalpy change that occurs when a certain number of moles of reactants as indicated by the balanced chemical equation, react together completely to give the products under standard conditions, i.e 25 °C (298K) and one atmosphere pressure. All the reactants and products must be in their standard physical states. Its units are kJ mol-1 .

questions.text: An azeotropic mixture of two liquids has a boiling point higher than either of them when it:

options.text: Shows positive deviation from Raoult’s law
options.explanation: In positive deviation, the partial pressure of each component that is present in the solution is greater than the vapour pressure as expected or they have a lower boiling point than the expected value. In this type of deviation, the bond between solute-solvent is weaker than the bond present between solute-solute and solvent-solvent. So, it is easy for them to break the bonds at lower temperatures and hence, have a lower boiling point. The azeotropic solutions which show positive deviation are known as a minimum boiling azeotrope.

options.text: Shows negative deviation from Raoult’s law
options.explanation: Negative deviations are those in which the partial vapour pressure of each component of a solution is less than the expected value of it. For example, the solution of acetone and chloroform. The bond between solute-solvent is stronger than the bond present between solute-solute and solvent-solvent. So, it is difficult for them to break the bonds at lower temperatures and hence, have a high boiling point. The azeotropic solutions which show negative deviation are known as a maximum boiling azeotrope.
Whereas the solution which obeys Roult's law is known as the ideal solution and does not show any deviation in the vapour pressure and boiling point.

questions.text: Ascorbic acid (vitamin C) contains 40.92% carbon, 4.58% hydrogen and 54.5% of oxygen by mass. What is the empirical formula of ascorbic acid?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The correct option is (a) C3H4O3, as it matches the empirical formula derived from the given percentages of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen.
This formula does not have the same ratios as the given percentages. It suggests a higher proportion of oxygen compared to the given data.
This formula suggests only one carbon atom, which is not in agreement with the given percentage of carbon.
This formula suggests six carbon atoms, which is not consistent with the given data.
This formula suggests two carbon atoms, which does not match the given percentage of carbon.

questions.explanation: The empirical formula is the ratio between the number of atoms of different elements which is present in the molecule of the given compound. Empirical formulas usually give the proportion of the elements present in the molecule of the given compound but not the actual number of the atoms or how the atoms are arranged. Complete step by step answer: If the mass of the compound is given then the empirical formula is calculated by changing the mass of every element to moles and then by dividing every mole value by the lowest value of mole computed and then the value of mole is rounded off to the closest whole number. Given in the question: 100% of vitamin C contains 40.92% carbon by mass, 4.58% hydrogen, and 54.50% oxygen Or we can say that 100 g of vitamin C contains 40.92 g carbon by mass, 4.58 g hydrogen, and 54.50 g oxygen. The atomic mass of carbon = 12 g/mol The atomic mass of hydrogen = 1 g/mol The atomic mass of oxygen = 16 g/mol The number of moles is calculated by the ratio of the given mass to the molar mass. Number of moles of carbon = 40.92\12 = 3.4 mole Number of moles of hydrogen = 4.58\1 = 4.58 mole Number of moles of oxygen = 54.50\16= 3.40 mole The molar ratio of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen is: C:H:O 3.4 : 4.58 : 3.4 = 3:4:3 C3H4O3 So, the correct answer is “Option A”. Note: If the molecular formula of a compound is given then to calculate the empirical formula from the molecular formula we have to divide all the subscripts in the empirical formula by the simplest whole number. The molecular formula is the actual whole number ratio of the elements present in the molecule of the compound.

The correct option (d) reflects the correct trend of reactivity in SN2 reactions, where alkyl iodides (RI) are the most reactive followed by alkyl bromides (RBr), alkyl chlorides (RCl), and alkyl fluorides (RF) in decreasing order of reactivity.

questions.explanation: The reactivity of the alkyl halide in the SN2 reaction is decided by the ease with which the halide leaves the substrate. The larger the size of the halide ion, the more is the leaving ability. As per the leaving ability, the order is I−>Br−>Cl−>F-. Hence reactivity order is RI > RBr > RCl > RF due to the higher stability of I- ion (due to its higher size). Hence, I ion is easily displaced by a nucleophile.

questions.text: The major source of unburnt hydrocarbons in the atmosphere is/are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Petroleum is a fossil fuel composed of hydrocarbons and is used as a source of energy. It is refined to produce various fuels such as gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel. When petroleum-based fuels are burned, hydrocarbons can be released into the atmosphere. However, petroleum itself is not the major source of unburnt hydrocarbons; it is the burning of petroleum fuels that contributes to the release of hydrocarbons.

Natural gas is another fossil fuel composed mainly of methane, which is a hydrocarbon. Like petroleum, the burning of natural gas can release hydrocarbons into the atmosphere. However, natural gas is generally considered to have lower emissions compared to other fossil fuels, such as coal and oil. Therefore, while natural gas can contribute to hydrocarbon emissions, it is not the major source of unburnt hydrocarbons.

Automobiles, especially those powered by internal combustion engines, are a significant source of unburnt hydrocarbons in the atmosphere. When fuel is burned in car engines, incomplete combustion can occur, leading to the release of unburnt hydrocarbons into the air. This is especially true for older vehicles or those with poorly maintained engines and exhaust systems. In recent years, efforts have been made to reduce automobile emissions and improve engine efficiency to minimize the release of unburnt hydrocarbons.

While human beings do contribute to hydrocarbon emissions through activities such as cooking, heating, and the use of certain products like solvents and aerosols, the emissions from these sources are typically much lower compared to the emissions from automobiles and industrial processes. Therefore, while human activities can release hydrocarbons into the atmosphere, they are not the major source of unburnt hydrocarbons.

questions.explanation: There are many sources of unburned hydrocarbons. Unburned hydrocarbons (UHC) are typically an output of incomplete combustion due to unfavorable engine design, low fuel quality, or failure in the control system. Sometimes unburned hydrocarbons are released into the air during incomplete combustion. Burning fossil fuels, including gasoline in automobile engines, releases some hydrocarbons into the air. In a typical urban environment, the atmospheric concentration of hydrocarbons is around 3 ppm (parts per million).

questions.text: Enzymes that are functioning within the cell are called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Endoenzymes are enzymes that act within the cell, and their catalytic activity is confined to the interior of the cell. They are involved in intracellular processes, catalyzing reactions within the cell's cytoplasm or organelles. Enzymes involved in glycolysis, the Krebs cycle, or other metabolic pathways taking place inside the cell are considered endoenzymes.

Exoenzymes are enzymes that act outside the cell. They are synthesized within the cell but are then released into the extracellular environment to catalyze reactions there. They play a role in breaking down large molecules into smaller ones outside the cell, facilitating the uptake of nutrients. Digestive enzymes, such as amylases and lipases, are often exoenzymes. They are produced by cells in the pancreas or salivary glands but function in the extracellular space of the digestive system.

Holoenzyme is a complete, functional enzyme, which is catalytically active. Holoenzyme consists of an apoenzyme together with its cofactors. Holoenzyme contains all the subunits required for the functioning of an enzyme, e.g. DNA polymerase III, RNA polymerase. Holoenzyme = Apoenzyme + Cofactor.

Endoenzymes are enzymes that act within the cell, and their catalytic activity is confined to the interior of the cell. They are involved in intracellular processes, catalyzing reactions within the cell's cytoplasm or organelles. Enzymes involved in glycolysis, the Krebs cycle, or other metabolic pathways taking place inside the cell are considered endoenzymes. A holoenzyme is a fully functional enzyme that consists of both a protein component, known as the apoenzyme, and a non-protein component, referred to as the cofactor. The term "holoenzyme" reflects the complete and active form of the enzyme.

questions.explanation: Endoenzymes are enzymes that act within the cell, and their catalytic activity is confined to the interior of the cell. They are involved in intracellular processes, catalyzing reactions within the cell's cytoplasm or organelles. Enzymes involved in glycolysis, the Krebs cycle, or other metabolic pathways taking place inside the cell are considered endoenzymes.

questions.text: Which of the following fertilizers has the maximum percentage of nitrogen in solid state?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ammonia (NH3) has a molar mass of 17 g/mol, with nitrogen contributing 14 g/mol. The percentage of nitrogen in ammonia is calculated as:Percentage of N = (mass of N / total mass) x 100 = (14 / 17) x 100 ≈ 82.35%
Urea (NH2CONH2) has a molar mass of 60.06 g/mol. The nitrogen content is:Percentage of N = (2 x 14.006 / 60.06) x 100 ≈ 46.6%Thus, urea contains significantly less nitrogen than ammonia.

Diammonium hydrogen phosphate ((NH4)2HPO4) has a molar mass of 132 g/mol. The nitrogen content is calculated as:Percentage of N = (2 x 14 / 132) x 100 ≈ 10.6%This indicates that it contains much less nitrogen than ammonia.

Ammonium nitrate (NH4NO3) has a molar mass of 80.04 g/mol. The nitrogen content is:Percentage of N = (2 x 14 / 80.04) x 100 ≈ 34.9%While this is a significant nitrogen content, it is still lower than that of ammonia.

questions.explanation: Urea contains 46% nitrogen. the highest among common solid nitrogen fertilizers. Ammonium nitrate has about 34% N. and diammonium hydrogen phosphate has even less. Ammonia is not used as a solid fertilizer (it's typically gaseous or liquid), so urea gives the maximum solid-state nitrogen content.

questions.text: To avoid the formation of toxic compounds with chlorine, which substance is used for disinfecting water?

Ozone (O3) is a powerful oxidizing agent that effectively disinfects water without forming harmful compounds with chlorine. It is a preferred choice to avoid the formation of toxic byproducts.
Alum is used primarily for coagulation in water treatment processes, helping to remove suspended particles. It does not specifically prevent toxic reactions with chlorine.

questions.explanation: Ozone (O3) is the correct answer because it functions as a strong disinfectant that does not produce harmful byproducts when used alongside chlorine. Unlike chloramines, which still involve chlorine and can lead to the formation of toxic compounds, ozone helps in eliminating pathogens effectively without the same risks. Potassium permanganate and alums, while useful in water treatment, do not specifically address the concern of chlorine-related toxicity. Thus, ozone serves as a safer alternative in disinfection processes.

questions.text: How much heat is absorbed by 100 g of water when its temperature decreases from 25oC to 5oC? (Heat capacity is 4.2 J/g K)

To calculate the heat absorbed by a substance, we can use the formula:q = m * C * ΔTWhere:q is the heat absorbed or released,m is the mass of the substance,C is the specific heat capacity of the substance, andΔT is the change in temperature.In this case, we are calculating the heat absorbed by 100 g of water when its temperature decreases from 25°C to 5°C, and the specific heat capacity of water is given as 4.2 J/g·K.ΔT = (Final temperature - Initial temperature) = (278 - 298°C) = -20KNote: The change in temperature is negative because the water is decreasing in temperature.Now we can plug in the values into the formula:q = 100 g * 4.2 J/g·K * (-20K)q = -8400 JThe negative sign indicates that heat is being released or lost by the water. Therefore, 100 g of water releases 84,00 J of heat when its temperature decreases from 25°C to 5°C.

questions.text: If a wave can be polarized, it means, it is:

questions.explanation: Only transverse waves can be polarised. Longitudinal waves cannot be polarised.Transverse: vibrations perpendicular to the direction of travel of the wave.Longitudinal: vibrations parallel to the direction of travel of the wave.Sound waves are longitudinal so they cannot be polarised.Polarised: the wave vibrations occur in one plane onlyUnpolarised: the wave vibrations occur in all planes perpendicular to the direction of travel.The reason that only transverse waves can be polarised is that their vibrations can potentially occur in all directions perpendicular to the direction of travel. It is therefore possible to confine the vibrations to a single plane. Or put another way an unpolarised transverse wave is a 3D shape, it is therefore possible to confine it to a 2D shape (a plane). The vibrations of a longitudinal wave occur along a single line, it is therefore not possible to confine that to a plane, i.e. a 1D shape cannot be confined within a 2D shape. Similarly, Stationary waves and superimposed waves can not be polarised as they are along a single line.

questions.text: The phenomenon by which light travels in an optical fiber is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.topic: Optics, Nature of Light and Optical Instruments

options.explanation: While light does "reflect" inside the fiber, "Reflection" as a general term usually refers to light bouncing off a surface (like a standard glass mirror). Standard reflection often involves some absorption or "leakage" of light. In an optical fiber, we need the total preservation of the signal, which is specifically described by the physics of Total Internal Reflection.

Refraction is the bending of light as it passes from one transparent substance into another (like light bending when it enters water). In an optical fiber, refraction is actually what we want to avoid. If the light hits the boundary at an angle smaller than the critical angle, it will refract out of the core and into the cladding, causing the signal to escape and be lost.

Total Internal Reflection (TIR) is the specific process that allows light to travel long distances through a fiber without losing its intensity to the surrounding environment.For TIR to occur, two conditions must be met:Density Difference: Light must travel from a medium with a higher refractive index to one with a lower refractive index (n cladding. Critical Angle: The angle at which the light hits the boundary (the "angle of incidence") must be greater than a specific value called the critical angle.When these conditions are met, 100% of the light is reflected back into the core, acting like a perfect mirror. This allows the signal to "zig-zag" down the cable over kilometers with minimal loss.

Transmission is a broad term that describes the act of light (or any wave) passing through a medium or a system. While it is true that an optical fiber "transmits" data, transmission is the result, not the physical phenomenon. It doesn't explain how the light is kept inside the glass strand as it curves.

questions.explanation: Total internal reflection is the phenomenon by which light propagates through an optical fiber. When light enters the fiber at an angle greater than the critical angle, it undergoes total internal reflection, meaning that it reflects back into the fiber rather than refracting out. This bouncing of light within the fiber allows it to travel long distances without significant loss of signal strength.

questions.text: The entropy of the universe always:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A (Decreases) is incorrect because it contradicts the second law of thermodynamics, which states that entropy tends to increase, not decrease.

The energy of the universe increases because energy never flows uphill spontaneously. Energy always flows downhill, and this causes an increase in entropy. Entropy is the spreading out of energy, and energy tends to spread out as much as possible. It flows spontaneously from a hot (i.e. highly energetic) region to a cold (less energetic) region. As a result, energy becomes evenly distributed across the two regions, and the temperature of the two regions becomes equal. The same thing happens on a much larger scale. The Sun and every other star are radiating energy into the universe. Mathematically, consider the total energy q that is transferred from a hot region at temperature T1 to a cold region at temperature T2. The entropy S1 of the hot region is defined as S1=qT1 The entropy S2 of the cold region is S2=qT2 Therefore, during the energy transfer, the change in entropy is ΔS=S2−S1 =qT2−qT1 =q(T2−T1) Since T2<T1,1T2>1T1​ and ΔS is positive. The total entropy of the system increases. Since energy never flows spontaneously in the other direction, the total entropy of the universe is always increasing.
Option C (Stays the same) is incorrect because it contradicts the second law of thermodynamics, which predicts that entropy tends to increase over time, not remain constant.

Option D (Both A and B) is incorrect because the entropy of the universe always increases according to the second law of thermodynamics. While there may be local decreases in entropy (such as in the formation of ordered structures), the overall trend is towards increasing entropy. Therefore, option B (Increases) is the correct choice.
